unit.comment: /*************************************************************************** * _ _ ____ _ * Project ___| | | | _ \| | * / __| | | | |_) | | * | (__| |_| | _ <| |___ * \___|\___/|_| \_\_____| * * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al. * * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html. * * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file. * * This software is distributed on an "AS IS" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY * KIND, either express or implied. * * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl * ***************************************************************************/
/* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */
/* MSDOS/Windows style drive prefix, eg c: in c:foo */
/* MSDOS/Windows style drive prefix, optionally with * a '|' instead of ':', followed by a slash or NUL */
/* scheme is not URL encoded, the longest libcurl supported ones are... */
/* * If ENABLE_IPV6 is disabled, we still want to parse IPv6 addresses, so make * sure we have _some_ value for AF_INET6 without polluting our fake value * everywhere. */
/* Internal representation of CURLU. Point to URL-encoded strings. */

function.name: free_urlhandle

unit.comment: /* * Find the separator at the end of the host name, or the '?' in cases like * http://www.example.com?id=2380 */

function.name: find_host_sep

unit.comment: /* convert CURLcode to CURLUcode */

/* urlencode_str() writes data into an output dynbuf and URL-encodes the * spaces in the source URL accordingly. * * URL encoding should be skipped for host names, otherwise IDN resolution * will fail. */

function.name: urlencode_str
function.parameter_list: (struct dynbuf *o, const char *url, size_t len, bool relative, bool query)

unit.comment: /* * Returns the length of the scheme if the given URL is absolute (as opposed * to relative). Stores the scheme in the buffer if TRUE and 'buf' is * non-NULL. The buflen must be larger than MAX_SCHEME_LEN if buf is set. * * If 'guess_scheme' is TRUE, it means the URL might be provided without * scheme. */

function.name: Curl_is_absolute_url
function.parameter_list: (const char *url, char *buf, size_t buflen, bool guess_scheme)

unit.comment: /* * Concatenate a relative URL to a base URL making it absolute. * URL-encodes any spaces. * The returned pointer must be freed by the caller unless NULL * (returns NULL on out of memory). * * Note that this function destroys the 'base' string. */

function.name: concat_url

unit.comment: /* scan for byte values <= 31, 127 and sometimes space */

function.name: junkscan

unit.comment: /* * parse_hostname_login() * * Parse the login details (user name, password and options) from the URL and * strip them out of the host name * */

function.name: parse_hostname_login
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_URL *u, const char *login, size_t len, unsigned int flags, size_t *offset)

function.name: Curl_parse_port

unit.comment: /* this assumes 'hostname' now starts with [ */

function.name: ipv6_parse
hostname_check

unit.comment: /* * Handle partial IPv4 numerical addresses and different bases, like * '16843009', '0x7f', '0x7f.1' '0177.1.1.1' etc. * * If the given input string is syntactically wrong IPv4 or any part for * example is too big, this function returns HOST_NAME. * * Output the "normalized" version of that input string in plain quad decimal * integers. * * Returns the host type. */
/* out of memory */
/* bad IPv4 address */

function.name: ipv4_normalize

unit.comment: /* if necessary, replace the host content with a URL decoded version */

function.name: urldecode_host
parse_authority
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_URL *u, const char *auth, size_t authlen, unsigned int flags, struct dynbuf *host, bool has_scheme)

function.name: Curl_url_set_authority

unit.comment: /* * "Remove Dot Segments" * https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3986#section-5.2.4 */

/* * dedotdotify() * @unittest: 1395 * * This function gets a null-terminated path with dot and dotdot sequences * passed in and strips them off according to the rules in RFC 3986 section * 5.2.4. * * The function handles a query part ('?' + stuff) appended but it expects * that fragments ('#' + stuff) have already been cut off. * * RETURNS * * Zero for success and 'out' set to an allocated dedotdotified string. */
unit.function_decl: UNITTEST int dedotdotify(const char *input, size_t clen, char **outp);

function.name: dedotdotify
parseurl
parseurl_and_replace
curl_url
curl_url_cleanup

unit.cpp:define: #define DUP(dest, src, name) \ do { \ if(src->name) { \ dest->name = strdup(src->name); \ if(!dest->name) \ goto fail; \ } \ } while(0)

function.name: curl_url_dup
curl_url_get
function.parameter_list: (const CURLU *u, CURLUPart what, char **part, unsigned int flags)

function.name: curl_url_set
function.parameter_list: (CURLU *u, CURLUPart what, const char *part, unsigned int flags)
